name: xcrun
description: Find and execute the named command line tool from the active xCode developer directory

subcommands.name: scntool
subcommands.description: SceneKit CLI utilities

name: --convert
options.description: File to convert

name: --format
options.description: Format to convert to

name: --output
options.description: Path to the output file

name: --force-y-up
options.description: Convert objects to use y axis up

name: --force-interleaved
options.description: Interleave the vertex data for multiple semantics in the same array to achieve better rendering performance

name: --prefer-compressed-textures
options.description: Use .ktx, .astc and .pvrtc files for textures if available in the asset catalog

name: --verbose
options.description: Get pretty error message

name: -usage
options.description: Print brief usage

name: -help
options.description: Print complete usage

name: -verbose
options.description: Provide additional status output

name: -license
options.description: Show the Xcode and SDK license agreements

name: -checkFirstLaunchStatus
options.description: Check if any First Launch tasks need to be performed

name: -runFirstLaunch
options.description: Install packages and agree to the license

name: -project
options.description: Build the project NAME

name: -target
options.description: Build the target NAME

name: -alltargets
options.description: Build all targets

name: -workspace
options.description: Build the workspace NAME

name: -scheme
options.description: Build the scheme NAME

name: -configuration
options.description: Use the build configuration NAME for building each target

name: -xcconfig
options.description: Apply the build settings defined in the file at PATH as overrides

name: -arch
options.description: Build each target for the architecture ARCH; this will override architectures defined in the project

name: -sdk
options.description: Use SDK as the name or path of the base SDK when building the project

name: -toolchain
options.description: Use the toolchain with identifier or name NAME

name: -destination
options.description: Use the destination described by DESTINATIONSPECIFIER (a comma-separated set of key=value pairs describing the destination to use)

name: -destination-timeout
options.description: Wait for TIMEOUT seconds while searching for the destination device

name: -parallelizeTargets
options.description: Build independent targets in parallel

name: -jobs
options.description: Specify the maximum number of concurrent build operations

name: -maximum-concurrent-test-device-destinations
options.description: The maximum number of device destinations to test on concurrently

name: -maximum-concurrent-test-simulator-destinations
options.description: The maximum number of simulator destinations to test on concurrently

name: -parallel-testing-enabled
options.description: Overrides the per-target setting in the scheme

name: -parallel-testing-worker-count
options.description: The exact number of test runners that will be spawned during parallel testing

name: -maximum-parallel-testing-workers
options.description: The maximum number of test runners that will be spawned during parallel testing

name: -dry-run
options.description: Do everything except actually running the commands

name: -quiet
options.description: Do not print any output except for warnings and errors

name: -hideShellScriptEnvironment
options.description: Don't show shell script environment variables in build log

name: -showsdks
options.description: Display a compact list of the installed SDKs

name: -showdestinations
options.description: Display a list of destinations

name: -showTestPlans
options.description: Display a list of test plans

name: -showBuildSettings
options.description: Display a list of build settings and values

name: -showBuildSettingsForIndex
options.description: Display build settings for the index service

name: -list
options.description: Lists the targets and configurations in a project, or the schemes in a workspace

name: -find-executable
options.description: Display the full path to executable NAME in the provided SDK and toolchain

name: -find-library
options.description: Display the full path to library NAME in the provided SDK and toolchain

name: -version
options.description: Display the version of Xcode; with -sdk will display info about one or all installed SDKs

name: -enableAddressSanitizer
options.description: Turn the address sanitizer on or off

name: -enableThreadSanitizer
options.description: Turn the thread sanitizer on or off

name: -enableUndefinedBehaviorSanitizer
options.description: Turn the undefined behavior sanitizer on or off

name: -resultBundlePath
options.description: Specifies the directory where a result bundle describing what occurred will be placed

name: -resultStreamPath
options.description: Specifies the file where a result stream will be written to (the file must already exist)

name: -resultBundleVersion
options.description: Specifies which result bundle version should be used

name: -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath
options.description: Specifies the directory to which remote source packages are fetch or expected to be found

name: -derivedDataPath
options.description: Specifies the directory where build products and other derived data will go

name: -archivePath
options.description: Specifies the directory where any created archives will be placed, or the archive that should be exported

name: -exportArchive
options.description: Specifies that an archive should be exported

name: -exportNotarizedApp
options.description: Export an archive that has been notarized by Apple

name: -exportOptionsPlist
options.description: Specifies a path to a plist file that configures archive exporting

name: -enableCodeCoverage
options.description: Turn code coverage on or off when testing

name: -exportPath
options.description: Specifies the destination for the product exported from an archive

name: -skipUnavailableActions
options.description: Specifies that scheme actions that cannot be performed should be skipped instead of causing a failure

name: -exportLocalizations
options.description: Exports completed and outstanding project localizations

name: -importLocalizations
options.description: Imports localizations for a project, assuming any necessary localized resources have been created in Xcode

name: -localizationPath
options.description: Specifies a path to XLIFF localization file

name: -exportLanguage
options.description: Specifies multiple optional ISO 639-1 languages included in a localization export

name: -xcroot
options.description: Specifies a path to a .xcroot to use for building and/or testing

name: -xctestrun
options.description: Specifies a path to a test run specification

name: -testPlan
options.description: Specifies the name of the test plan associated with the scheme to use for testing

name: -only-testing
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying tests to include, and excluding other tests

name: -only-testing:
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying tests to include, and excluding other tests

name: -skip-testing
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying tests to exclude, but including other tests

name: -skip-testing:
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying tests to exclude, but including other tests

name: -test-timeouts-enabled
options.description: Enable or disable test timeout behavior

name: -default-test-execution-time-allowance
options.description: The default execution time an individual test is given to execute, if test timeouts are enabled

name: -maximum-test-execution-time-allowance
options.description: The maximum execution time an individual test is given to execute, regardless of the test's preferred allowance

name: -only-test-configuration
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying test configurations to include, and excluding other test configurations

name: -skip-test-configuration
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying test configurations to exclude, but including other test configurations

name: -testLanguage
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying ISO 639-1 language in which to run the tests

name: -testRegion
options.description: Constrains testing by specifying ISO 3166-1 region in which to run the tests

name: -resolvePackageDependencies
options.description: Resolves any Swift package dependencies referenced by the project or workspace

name: -disableAutomaticPackageResolution
options.description: Prevents packages from automatically being resolved to versions other than those recorded in the `Package.resolved` file

name: -json
options.description: Output as JSON

name: -allowProvisioningUpdates
options.description: Allow xcodebuild to communicate with the Apple Developer website. For automatically signed targets, xcodebuild will create and update profiles, app IDs, and certificates. For manually signed targets, xcodebuild will download missing or updated provisioning profiles. Requires a developer account to have been added in Xcode's Accounts preference pane

name: -allowProvisioningDeviceRegistration
options.description: Allow xcodebuild to register your destination device on the developer portal if necessary. This flag only takes effect if -allowProvisioningUpdates is also passed

name: -scmProvider
options.description: Which implementation to use for Git operations (system/xcode)

name: -showBuildTimingSummary
options.description: Display a report of the timings of all the commands invoked during the build

name: -create-xcframework
options.description: Create an xcframework from prebuilt libraries; -help for more information

name: build
options.description: Build the target in the build root (SYMROOT).  This is the default build action

name: installsrc
options.description: Copy the source of the project to the source root (SRCROOT)

name: install
options.description: Build the target and install it into the target's installation directory in the distribution root (DSTROOT)

name: clean
options.description: Remove build products and intermediate files from the build root (SYMROOT)

subcommands.name: addmedia
subcommands.description: Add photos, live photos, videos, or contacts to the library of a device

subcommands.name: boot
subcommands.description: Boot a device or device pair

name: --arch
options.description: Specify the architecture to use when booting the simulator (eg: 'arm64' or 'x86_64')

name: --disabledJob
options.description: Disables the given launchd job. Multiple jobs can be disabled by passing multiple flags

name: --enabledJob
options.description: Enables the given launchd job when it would normally be disabled. Multiple jobs can be enabled by passing multiple flags

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: Clone an existing device

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new device

args.name: device type id
args.description: A valid available device type. Find these by running 'xcrun simctl list devicetypes'. Examples: ('iPhone X', 'com.apple.CoreSimulator.SimDeviceType.iPhone-X')

args.name: runtime id
args.description: A valid and available runtime. Find these by running 'xcrun simctl list runtimes'. If no runtime is specified the newest runtime compatible with the device type is chosen. Examples: ('watchOS3', 'watchOS3.2', 'watchOS 3.2', 'com.apple.CoreSimulator.SimRuntime.watchOS-3-2', '/Volumes/path/to/Runtimes/watchOS 3.2.simruntime')

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete specified devices, unavailable devices, or all devices

args.name: unavailable
args.description: Specifying unavailable will delete devices that are not supported by the current Xcode SDK

subcommands.name: diagnose
subcommands.description: Collect diagnostic information and logs

name: -b
options.description: Do NOT show the resulting archive in a Finder window upon completion

name: -X
options.description: Run all diagnostics with no timeout. It ignores the --timeout value if it is specified

name: --timeout
options.description: Specify a duration (in seconds) to wait for the log collection before timeout

name: --output
options.description: Specify the output directory. If not provided the temporary directory is used

name: --no-archive
options.description: Do not create an archive, leave the collected files uncompressed

name: --all-logs
options.description: Include all device logs, even for non-booted devices

name: --data-containers
options.description: Include booted device(s) data directory. Warning: May include private information, app data containers, and increases the size of the archive! Default is NOT to collect the data container

name: --udid
options.description: Collect diagnostics only from the specified device. This option may be specified more than once to consider multiple devices. The --all-logs option causes all --udid options to be ignored

subcommands.name: erase
subcommands.description: Erase a device's contents and settings

args.name: all
args.description: Specifying all will erase all existing devices

subcommands.name: get_app_container
subcommands.description: Print the path of the installed app's container

args.name: container
args.description:
Optionally specify the container. Defaults to app.
app                 The .app bundle
data                The application's data container
groups              The App Group containers
<group identifier>  A specific App Group container

subcommands.name: getenv
subcommands.description: Print an environment variable from a running device

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Prints the usage for a given subcommand

subcommands.name: icloud_sync
subcommands.description: Trigger iCloud sync on a device

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install an app on a device

subcommands.name: install_app_data
subcommands.description: Install an xcappdata package to a device, replacing the current contents of the container

subcommands.name: io
subcommands.description: Set up a device IO operation

subcommands.name: enumerate
subcommands.description: Lists all available IO ports and descriptor states

name: --poll
options.description: Poll after enumeration

subcommands.name: poll
subcommands.description: Polls all available IO ports for events

subcommands.name: recordVideo
subcommands.description: Records the display to a QuickTime movie at the specified file or url

name: --codec
options.description: Specifies the codec type: 'h264' or 'hevc'. Default is 'hevc'

name: --display
options.description: iOS: supports 'internal' or 'external'. Default is 'internal'. tvOS: supports only 'external' watchOS: supports only 'internal'

name: --mask
options.description: For non-rectangular displays, handle the mask by policy: ignored: The mask is ignored and the unmasked framebuffer is saved. alpha: Not supported, but retained for compatibility; the mask is rendered black. black: The mask is rendered black

name: --force
options.description: Force the output file to be written to, even if the file already exists

subcommands.name: screenshot
subcommands.description: Saves a screenshot as a PNG to the specified file or url(use '-' for stdout)

name: --type
options.description: Can be 'png', 'tiff', 'bmp', 'gif', 'jpeg'. Default is png

name: --display
options.description: iOS: supports 'internal' or 'external'. Default is 'internal'. tvOS: supports only 'external' watchOS: supports only 'internal'. You may also specify a port by UUID

name: --mask
options.description: For non-rectangular displays, handle the mask by policy: ignored: The mask is ignored and the unmasked framebuffer is saved. alpha: The mask is used as premultiplied alpha. black: The mask is rendered black

subcommands.name: keychain
subcommands.description: Manipulate a device's keychain

subcommands.name: add-root-cert
subcommands.description: Add a certificate to the trusted root store

subcommands.name: add-cert
subcommands.description: Add a certificate to the keychain

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset the keychain

subcommands.name: launch
subcommands.description: Launch an application by identifier on a device

name: --console
options.description: Block and print the application's stdout and stderr to the current terminal. Signals received by simctl are passed through to the application. (Cannot be combined with --stdout or --stderr)

name: --console-pty
options.description: Block and print the application's stdout and stderr to the current terminal via a PTY. Signals received by simctl are passed through to the application. (Cannot be combined with --stdout or --stderr)

name: --stdout
options.description: Redirect the application's standard output to a file

name: --stderr
options.description: Redirect the application's standard error to a file

name: --terminate-running-process
options.description: Terminate any running copy of the application. Note: Log output is often directed to stderr, not stdout

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List available devices, device types, runtimes, or device pairs

name: -j
--json
options.description: Print as JSON

name: -e
--no-escape-slashes
options.description: Encode slashes without escapes in JSON output

name: -v
options.description: More verbose output

subcommands.name: location
subcommands.description: Control a device's simulated location

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List available simulation scenarios

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: Stop any running scenario and clear any simulated location

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set the location to a specific latitude and longitude

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a simulated location scenario (use the list action to get a list of scenarios)

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description:
Set the location to a series of waypoints specified as 'lat,lon' pairs, interpolating between them over time.
At least two waypoints are required. Use '-' to read waypoints from stdin, one waypoint per li
Speed specifies how quickly to move between waypoints in meters per second. If not specified 20m/s is us
The system will issue location updates along the path between each pair of waypoints. Use distance or interval to 
control how often those updates are issued. Distance will issue an update every <meters> travelled without regard
for the time between updates. Interval will issue updates at fixed times without regard for how much
the location moves between updates.
If neither are specified an interval of 1.0 seconds is used. If both are specified the system picks on
Example simulating a direct line between San Francisco and New York City, with updates every km:
set --distance=1000 --speed=260 37.629538,-122.395733 40.628083,-73.768254

subcommands.name: logverbose
subcommands.description:
Enable or disable verbose logging for a device.
NOTE: You may need to reboot the affected device before logging changes will be effective

args.name: device
args.description: The device. If not provided all booted devices are affected

subcommands.name: openurl
subcommands.description: Open a URL in a device

subcommands.name: pair
subcommands.description: Create a new watch and phone pair

subcommands.name: pair_activate
subcommands.description: Set a given pair as active

subcommands.name: pbcopy
subcommands.description: Copy standard input onto the device pasteboard

subcommands.name: pbpaste
subcommands.description: Print the contents of the device's pasteboard to standard output

subcommands.name: pbsync
subcommands.description: Sync the pasteboard content from one pasteboard to another

name: -p
options.description: Causes simctl to use promise data for secondary types.  simctl will continue to run to provide that promise data until something else replaces it on the pasteboard

subcommands.name: privacy
subcommands.description: Grant, revoke, or reset privacy and permissions

args.name: bundle identifier
args.description: The bundle identifier of the target application

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Send a simulated push notification

args.name: bundle identifier
args.description: The bundle identifier of the target application If the payload file contains a 'Simulator Target Bundle' top-level key this parameter may be omitted. If both are provided this argument will override the value from the payload

args.name: json file
args.description:
Path to a JSON payload or '-' to read from stdin. The payload must:
- Contain an object at the top level.
- Contain an 'aps' key with valid Apple Push Notification values.
- Be 4096 bytes or less

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: Rename a device

subcommands.name: runtime
subcommands.description: Perform operations on runtimes

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a runtime disk image to the secure storage area. The image will be staged, verified, and mounted. When possible the image file will be cloned so no additional disk space will be used. If stdout is a terminal and a copy is required then progress will be reported

name: -m
--move
options.description: Remove the original file if the image is added successfully. If the image cannot be staged or the add fails the original is not removed

name: -a
--async
options.description: Print the UUID of the new image then exit, do not wait on the results of the add operation

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a simulator runtime from the secure storage area. If runtime is a disk image any booted simulators are shutdown and the disk is unmounted first. Use the alias 'all' to delete all images

name: -d
--notUsedSinceDays
options.description: Delete images not used within the past <days> days

name: -n
--dry-run
options.description: Print what images would be deleted without actually deleting anything

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.description: Re-verify the signature of a given runtime

name: -v
options.description: Print more verbose output

name: -j
--json
options.description: Print as JSON

subcommands.name: match list
subcommands.description: List the SDK build to runtime build mapping rules for the selected Xcode. Preferred means the runtime was either bundled with Xcode, exactly matched your SDK version, or the downloadable index indicated a better match for your SDK Manual overrides using 'match set' have the highest priority

name: -v
options.description: Verbose mode. Includes the full preferred build map, user override map, and known SDK names

name: -j
--json
options.description: Print as JSON

subcommands.name: match set
subcommands.description:
Override the SDK to runtime build mapping. This controls which build of a given runtime Xcode will prefer for building and running when using that SDK. This matters most often during Beta releases when there are multiple builds for a given OS version. If --sdkBuild is not specified it is assumed you mean the SDK build for the currently selected Xcode.

Note: Remember this is about build numbers, not semantic versions. When using the iOS 16.0 SDK Xcwill always prefer an iOS 16.0 runtime. Matching policy controls what to do when there are multiiOS 16.0 runtimes availabeg if the iOS 16.0 SDK is 20A245 and the available runtimes are (20A248, 20A252, 20A254) which should Xcode use for building, SwiftUI Previews, and when launching iOS 16.0 Simulators? They are all iOS 16.0 runtimes so a policy must decide which one is selected

name: --default
options.description: Clear the override for the given SDK and revert to default behavior

name: --sdkBuild
options.description: Explicitly specify the SDK build, eg for an Xcode other than the selected Xcode

subcommands.name: shutdown
subcommands.description: Shutdown a device

subcommands.name: spawn
subcommands.description: Spawn a process by executing a given executable on a device

subcommands.name: status_bar
subcommands.description: Set or clear status bar overrides

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List existing overrides

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: Clear all existing status bar overrides

subcommands.name: override
subcommands.description: Set status bar override values, according to these flags. You may specify any combination of these flags (at least one is required)

name: --time
options.description: Set the date or time to a fixed value. If the string is a valid ISO date string it will also set the date on relevant devices

name: --dataNetwork
options.description: If specified must be one of 'hide', 'wifi', '3g', '4g', 'lte', 'lte-a', 'lte+', '5g', '5g+', '5g-uwb', or '5g-uc'

name: --wifiMode
options.description: If specified must be one of 'searching', 'failed', or 'active'

name: --wifiBars
options.description: If specified must be 0-3

name: --cellularMode
options.description: If specified must be one of 'notSupported', 'searching', 'failed', or 'active'

name: --cellularBars
options.description: If specified must be 0-4

name: --operatorName
options.description: Set the cellular operator/carrier name. Use '' for the empty string

name: --batteryState
options.description: If specified must be one of 'charging', 'charged', or 'discharging'

name: --batteryLevel
options.description: If specified must be 0-100

subcommands.name: terminate
subcommands.description: Terminate an application by identifier on a device

subcommands.name: ui
subcommands.description: Get or Set UI options

subcommands.name: appearance
subcommands.description:
When invoked without arguments prints the current user interface appearance style:
light
The Light appearance style.
dark
The Dark appearance style.
unsupported
The platform or runtime version do not support appearance styles.
unknown
The current style is unknown or there was an error detecting it

subcommands.name: increase_contrast
subcommands.description:
When invoked without arguments prints whether the Increase Contrast mode is currently enabled:
enabled
Increase Contrast is enabled.
disabled
Increase Contrast is disabled.
unsupported
The platform or runtime version do not support the Increase Contrast setting.
unknown
The current setting is unknown or there was an error detecting it

subcommands.name: content_size
subcommands.description: When invoked without arguments prints the current preferred content size category, from the following possible values:

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstall an app from a device

subcommands.name: unpair
subcommands.description: Unpair a watch and phone pair

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade a device to a newer runtime

name: -h
--help
options.description: Help message

name: --version
options.description: Show the xcrun version

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Show verbose logging output

name: --sdk
options.description: Find the tool for the given SDK name

name: --toolchain
options.description: Find the tool for the given toolchain

name: -l
--log
options.description: Show command path to be executed (and --run)

name: -f
--find
options.description: Find and print the tool path

name: --run
options.description: (Default) find and execute the tool

name: -n
--no-cache
options.description: Do not use the lookup cache

name: -k
--kill-cache
options.description: Invalidate all existing cache entries

name: --show-sdk-path
options.description: Show selected SDK install path

name: --show-sdk-version
options.description: Show selected SDK version

name: --show-sdk-build-version
options.description: Show selected SDK build version

name: --show-sdk-platform-path
options.description: Show selected SDK platform path

name: --show-sdk-platform-version
options.description: Show selected SDK platform version
